name: Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation
dek: The flat, brown-to-violaceous patches that linger for months after a pimple resolves. More common, more persistent, and more under-treated in melanin-rich skin than the pigmentary concerns that get the airtime.
oneliner: Acne and injury aftermath. Faster to fade than melasma.
bottom: The condition with the highest treatment-discipline payoff in pigmentation. Most failed cases are sun-discipline failures, not molecule failures. Photograph at month 0 and month 4 — you will not believe the change otherwise.

triggers.k: Brown / dark patches at sites of past acne
triggers.n: Flat, sharply circumscribed. Not raised, not pink. The marker that tells you it is PIH and not active inflammation.
Spontaneous resolution is real but slow. Treatment compresses the timeline by months.

triggers.k: Worsens with sun exposure even briefly
triggers.n: UV is the single biggest accelerant. Daily SPF is the most consequential variable in any PIH protocol.

triggers.k: Triggered by friction, picking, or BPO sting
triggers.n: The inflammation does not need to be acne-shaped. Any trauma can produce a PIH plaque.

ingredients.name: Azelaic acid 10–15%
ingredients.evidence: First-line, pregnancy-safe, replicated 12-week PIH endpoints across skin types.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic 5% topical
ingredients.evidence: Replicated efficacy in skin of colour at 8–12 weeks. Pairs cleanly with azelaic.

ingredients.name: Niacinamide 4–10%
ingredients.evidence: Slows pigment hand-off from melanocyte to keratinocyte. Strong barrier credentials.

ingredients.name: Alpha arbutin 2%
ingredients.evidence: Gentler than hydroquinone, slower endpoints. Safe in pregnancy.

ingredients.name: Cysteamine 5%
ingredients.evidence: Promising in stubborn PIH; short contact protocol; smell remains the limiter.

ingredients.name: Hydroquinone 4%
ingredients.evidence: Reference compound. Cycle 3 months on, 2 off. Not in pregnancy.

phases.b: Mineral SPF 50+ daily, two finger-lengths. Ceramide moisturiser. No active layering yet — establish UV discipline first.
Azelaic 10% PM, build to nightly. Continue niacinamide AM. The 8-week mark is the earliest reasonable photo check.
Tranexamic 5% AM under SPF. Optional cysteamine introduction at week 12 if response plateaus.
Continue SPF and azelaic indefinitely; cycle hydroquinone or cysteamine in 3-month windows. Re-photograph every 8 weeks.
